photo_observations.cornice_details: no traditional cornice visible due to modern cladding

storefront_description: The ground floor is wrapped in a vivid tree-and-nature mural painted over corrugated metal, with a large white-framed storefront window cutting into the composition and turning the facade into a highly graphic retail frontage.

facade_description: 203 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed (metal cladding upper, corrugated metal lower) in a black board-and-batten upper, vibrant green/blue mural on corrugated metal ground floor palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using fixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s
